rules.message: `any` type used — defeats TypeScript's type checking for this value. Replace with a concrete type, a generic parameter, or `unknown` plus a type guard if the shape is genuinely dynamic. Restricted to actual type positions — a type annotation (`: any`), an `as any` cast, a generic argument opening with `any` (`<any`), or an `any[]` array type — so the bare word "any" in prose comments/strings (e.g. an ESLint disable comment naming `no-explicit-any`, or "works in any UI") no longer fires; comment lines are also skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Precision-limit residuals from this narrowing: a type-alias/generic-default RHS with no leading `:` (`type Payload = any;`, `function f<T = any>()`) is not matched, and a non-first generic argument (`Map<string, any>`) is not matched either — both are lexically indistinguishable from unrelated `=`/`,`-separated text without a real parser. Flags one finding per matching line, not per occurrence — a line with two `any`s still counts as a single finding. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule — test-only mocks/doubles routinely need `any`, and a loose type there is not a production type-safety bug). Suppress a vetted case with `// any-ok`.

`as` type cast used — bypasses TypeScript's structural checking for this expression. Prefer a type guard, a properly-typed API, or `unknown` plus a narrowing check. Import-alias `as` (e.g. `import { useState as useLocalState } from "react"`) does not count, nor does `as const` (a type-SAFETY idiom that narrows a literal instead of widening/escaping it, the opposite of a cast escape). A JSX `as` prop (e.g. `<Box as="span">`, common in polymorphic-component libraries like Mantine/styled-components) is also excluded via the `as=` attribute form; because the exclusion is line-level, ANY line containing an `as=` token (a JSX prop, but also an `as=` inside a string literal or a trailing inline comment) is skipped — so a genuine cast sharing such a line is missed. An accepted trade-off of the line-scan matcher. `as unknown as X` still counts — it is a genuine (double) cast escape. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`, so prose containing the word "as" in a full-line comment does not count; an inline trailing comment on a real code line is not covered by that skip. Flags one finding per matching line, not per occurrence — a line with two `as` casts still counts as a single finding. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule — same reasoning as this pack's `no-explicit-any`). Suppress a vetted case with `// as-ok`.

useEffect callback declared async — React silently ignores the returned Promise and no cleanup function can be returned (and a rejection becomes an unhandled rejection). Move the async work into an inner async function or IIFE instead: `useEffect(() => { void doAsync(); }, deps)`. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// unhandled-promise-ok`.

An `on<Event>={async ...}` JSX handler (or `on<Event>: async ...` handler-map property) has an `await` but no try/catch — a rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection. Add try/catch (or `.catch()`) inside the handler. Vetoed when a `try {` appears anywhere in the SAME enclosing symbol span: the parser projects a symbol span per top-level function/class-method, not per inline JSX-attribute arrow function, so this check runs over the whole enclosing function body, not just the handler's own body — a try/catch elsewhere in that same function (unrelated to this handler) also suppresses it, and an unrelated `on*={async ...}` handler sharing a function with an unrelated bare await can also co-trigger. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// async-handler-ok`.
